methodology: Counterfactual simulation using passc-prompt-audit counterfactual-analysis.json, gpt-pattern-audit error-catalog.json, and persistence-audit fresh-extract evidence. OPENAI_API_KEY unavailable — no live spot-check extractions.
proposedPromptSummary: Column-faithful extraction; pack notation as metadata; no inference from description; anti-phantom/lot guards; null over guessing

rows.product: Filete de Anchoas Alcofinosa L4 495 g
rows.reasoning: Description spelling variant only (Alcofinosa→Alconfirosa). Numerics correct. Column-faithful prompt does not address vision OCR on product names.
Description OCR noise (Uli→Uji). All numeric columns correct. Prompt redesign targets qty/price column fidelity, not name transcription.

rows.product: Atum Óleo Bolsa Nau Ciatrieta 1 Kg
rows.reasoning: Description OCR only. Qty=2, prices match GT. Not preventable by column-faithful rules.
Description accent/spelling noise. Qty=5 cx and prices correct. 6x1 L is pack metadata; column already authoritative at qty=5.
Primarily description OCR (Castelgrottto→Castagna). Fresh extract also shows minor unit_price drift (€9.92→€9.82). Column-faithful mandate may marginally reduce price bleed but name OCR persists.

Unit price €8.17→€17.06 is vision column bleed from weight range '4-4,25KG', not pack-inference. Proposed prompt strengthens column isolation but dense Italian grid misalignment is primarily a vision limitation; passc-prompt-audit rates this UNCERTAIN.
Description formatting variant only. All financial fields match GT. Not addressable by column-faithful redesign.

Qty truncated 2.56→2 and price inflated 15.06→19.32 while total ~preserved — classic wrong-column read, not pack multiplier. Column-only rule helps intent but fractional qty parsing on dense grids may still fail.

Qty 2→24 and unit_price €9.69→€0.85 caused by inferring 24-bottle pack from description over column qty=2. Proposed negative example explicitly forbids this. Fixing qty restores case-level unit and pricing.

Qty 6→2: YES — column shows 2, *6 is metadata (pass-c-raw had 6; fresh extract already qty=2). Price €25→€20 and total €50→€40: UNCERTAIN — persists in fresh extract despite correct qty; vision digit misread independent of pack inference. Row partially preventable.
Description OCR only (CABRA→CABEÇA). All numerics correct. Not preventable by prompt redesign.

rows.product: Aceto balsamico di Modena IGP pet 5l*2 Toschi
rows.reasoning: Qty 2→1: YES — column shows 1; *2 is pack metadata. Fresh extract (2026-06-11) already corrected qty=1 when inference suppressed, proving column is readable. Remaining price/total deltas (€18.929 vs €18.295) are separate column-read issues; qty error is prompt-preventable.

rows.product: MOZZA Fior di Latte Expert Julienne 3kg Simonetta
rows.reasoning: Description spelling (Fioc/Expet). Qty=10 and prices match GT. Not preventable.

Qty 2→1: YES — column shows 1; 1kg*2 is dual-pack metadata. Fresh extract corrected to qty=1. Line_total delta (€10.86 vs €10.38) may persist as price-column read issue, but primary pack-multiplier error is preventable.

Row absent from invoice (8 GT rows, geometry confirms 8). GPT fused lot 609 + Aceto 5l*2 cues. Proposed anti-phantom rules (reject Lote/Data Exp, no invented rows, one row per visible line) eliminate this row entirely.

summary.financialErrorsPrevented: 5/7 financially significant errors (pack multiplier + phantom) likely prevented; 2/7 column-shift errors uncertain; 0/8 OCR description errors prevented
summary.promptDrivenFixRate: 67% of financial field errors per passc-prompt-audit counterfactual (8/12 field-level verdicts YES)

bidfoodNote: Bidfood Portugal has zero errors in error-catalog.json — column-faithful prompt preserves all 11/11 correct rows (MO/EM units, column qty). No regression expected.
